MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-22-2017 IN 206 -APPEARANCES BY JOHN BILHEIMER, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF PENN VALLEY COMMUNITY CHURCH; MICHAEL PURSELL, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY -- DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 10/23/17 IS CONTINUED TO 3/5/18 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. ALL DEADLINES CURRENTLY CALCULATED BASED ON THE CURRENTLY SET TRIAL DATE OF 10/23/17, INCLUDING FACT AND EXPERT DISCOVERY AND EXPERT DISCLOSURES, WILL BE RECALCULATED PER THE CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE BASE ON THE NEW TRIAL DATE. IF DEFENDANT PLANS ON FILING A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, THEY MUST AKS FOR LEAVE OF COURT TO DO SO. LEAVE OF COURT IS NOT NECESSARY IF THE MOTIONS ARE BROUGHT FOR ANY NEW CAUSES OF ACTION ON AN AMENDMENT TO THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: GAIL DEKREON; CLERK: JIROQUE; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE SPECIFIC FACTS SHOWING "OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OF MALICE." (CIV. CODE 3294; SMITH V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1033, 1041; BROUSSEAU V. JARRETT (1977) 73 CAL.APP.3D 864, 872.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY MUST PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG. [302]

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The trial date in this case is June 25. It is past time the pleadings were settled. Plaintiff's second amended complaint seeks to plead a new claim for negligence against Brotherhood Mutual in its fourth cause of action - a claim not pled in the first amended complaint. Leave of court was not sought for this, so the demurrer to it is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (See CCP 473.) The SAC also pleads declaratory relief in a second cause of action which is substantively identical to the same claim pled in the FAC, but not previously demurred to. Such piecemeal demurrers are wasteful and improper, so the demurrer to that count is OVERRULED. (See Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2018) 7:34.4.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Deborah Neville, CSR No. 9703, e-mail: dneville@sftc.org (L&M302)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: The Court grants defendant's motion to strike. Plaintiff failed to allege specific facts showing "oppression, fraud, of malice." (Civ. Code 3294; Smith v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1033, 1041; Brousseau v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3d 864, 872.)

Counsel for defendant Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company must prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BROTHERHOOD MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

The Court sustains defendant Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company's demurrer to the sixth cause of action of the first amendment complaint with leave to amend. The Court sustains defendant's demurrer to the seventh cause of action without leave to amend.

As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiff fails to plead specific facts sufficient to establish the elements of fraud. (Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc. (2003) 30 Cal.4th 167, 184; Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) The complaint lacks particularized facts showing that Defendant did not intend to perform the promise at the time it was made. (Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30.) Plaintiff shall have ten days to file a second amended complaint to plead this cause of action if possible.

As to the seventh cause of action, prejudgment interest is a remedy not a cause of action. (Civ. Code, 3287 et seq.) Therefore, it is improperly pleaded as a cause of action. (Roberts v. Los Angeles County Bar Assn. (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 604, 618; Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 164.) Thus, the Court sustains the demurrer as to the seventh cause of action without leave to amend.

Counsel for defendant Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company must prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, email: rhunter@sftc.org.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2017 Continued To Mar-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF PENN VALLEY COMMUNITY CHURCH's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED and the trial date is continued to June 25, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206. Granting this motion is supported by California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The trial continuance, which is requested by defendant Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company, eliminates any possible prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (L&M302)
